file_name: GSITECHNOLOGYINC_11_16_2009-EX-10.2-INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY AGREEMENT between SONY ELECTRONICS INC. and GSI TECHNOLOGY, INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: 4. Each Party's Sole Right to the Intellectual Property Each Party shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to apply for and register for protection for, prosecute, and maintain any of its Intellectual Property and shall have the sole right to determine whether or not, and where, to apply for and/or register such protection, to abandon attempts to obtain protection or abandon registered protection of any Intellectual Property, and/or to discontinue the maintenance of any Intellectual Property without any obligation to inform the other Party of any such action or inaction. Neither Party is obligated to (a) file any patent application or to secure any patent or patent rights, or (b) maintain any patent in force or prosecute any patent application.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Each Party's Sole Right to the Intellectual Property Each Party shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to apply for and register for protection for, prosecute, and maintain any of its Intellectual Property and shall have the sole right to determine whether or not, and where, to apply for and/or register such protection, to abandon attempts to obtain protection or abandon registered protection of any Intellectual Property, and/or to discontinue the maintenance of any Intellectual Property with an obligation to inform the other Party of any such action or inaction. Neither Party is obligated to (a) file any patent application or to secure any patent or patent rights, or (b) maintain any patent in force or prosecute any patent application.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying Section 4 to state that each party *does* have an obligation to inform the other party before discontinuing maintenance of Intellectual Property, while the original text states there is no such obligation. This creates ambiguity about whether notification is required, causing uncertainty about enforcement. It now contradicts itself within the same section, making it unclear if informing the other party is mandatory or not. This change impacts the rights and responsibilities of both parties regarding intellectual property management.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party's Sole Right to the Intellectual Property Each Party shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to apply for and register for protection for, prosecute, and maintain any of its Intellectual Property and shall have the sole right to determine whether or not, and where, to apply for and/or register such protection, to abandon attempts to obtain protection or abandon registered protection of any Intellectual Property, and/or to discontinue the maintenance of any Intellectual Property with an obligation to inform the other Party of any such action or inaction. Neither Party is obligated to (a) file any patent application or to secure any patent or patent rights, or (b) maintain any patent in force or prosecute any patent application.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states "without any obligation to inform the other Party of any such action or inaction." The changed text modifies this to say "with an obligation to inform the other Party of any such action or inaction." This is a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section, leading to ambiguity and uncertainty about whether notification is required. The contradicted text being identical to the changed text simply indicates the contradiction is within that section itself.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Payment Consideration for the license granted by SONY under this IP Agreement is included in the Purchase Price, and PURCHASER shall have no obligation to make any additional payments to SONY for the license granted under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Payment Consideration for the license granted by SONY under this IP Agreement is included in the Purchase Price, and PURCHASER shall have an obligation to make additional payments to SONY for the license granted under this Agreement, as determined by SONY based on PURCHASER's revenue generated from the licensed IP.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in Section 6 by stating that PURCHASER *does* have an obligation to make additional payments to SONY, contradicting the original statement that PURCHASER has no such obligation. The additional payments are contingent upon PURCHASER's revenue generated from the licensed IP and are determined by SONY. This directly contradicts the first statement of the Payment section. This is in text because the original context dictates the payment terms, the contradiction directly opposes that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment Consideration for the license granted by SONY under this IP Agreement is included in the Purchase Price, and PURCHASER shall have an obligation to make additional payments to SONY for the license granted under this Agreement, as determined by SONY based on PURCHASER's revenue generated from the licensed IP.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 introduces a direct contradiction by stating that the PURCHASER *does* have an obligation to make additional payments to SONY, which directly conflicts with the original statement in the same section stating that the PURCHASER has no such obligation. This is a clear and impactful in-text contradiction as it concerns the fundamental payment terms of the agreement, rendering the payment clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 Termination (a) Should either Party fail to observe faithfully and materially perform each of the material obligations assumed by it in this IP Agreement for thirty (30) days after its attention has been directed to any such breach by notice in writing from the other Party, the non-breaching Party shall, at its option, have the right to terminate the license granted by it under this Agreement, such termination to be effected by serving notice in writing upon the breaching Party to that effect on or after the expiration of such period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 Termination (a) Should either Party fail to observe faithfully and materially perform each of the material obligations assumed by it in this IP Agreement for thirty (30) days after its attention has been directed to any such breach by notice in writing from the other Party, the non-breaching Party shall, at its option, have the right to terminate the license granted by the breaching party under this Agreement, such termination to be effected by serving notice in writing upon the breaching Party to that effect on or after the expiration of such period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 7.2(a) regarding which license can be terminated in the event of a breach. The original text states the non-breaching party can terminate *the license granted by it*, implying they terminate what they gave to the other party. The modified text states the non-breaching party can terminate *the license granted by the breaching party*, implying they can terminate what the breaching party gave to them. This causes confusion and uncertainty about which party's license is at risk upon a breach of the agreement. This contradiction can affect any section that refers to Section 7.2, where the termination conditions are mentioned, in text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination (a) Should either Party fail to observe faithfully and materially perform each of the material obligations assumed by it in this IP Agreement for thirty (30) days after its attention has been directed to any such breach by notice in writing from the other Party, the non-breaching Party shall, at its option, have the right to terminate the license granted by the breaching party under this Agreement, such termination to be effected by serving notice in writing upon the breaching Party to that effect on or after the expiration of such period of thirty (30) days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7.2(a) to state that in the event of a breach, the non-breaching party can terminate *the license granted by the breaching party*. However, the original text states that the non-breaching party can terminate *the license granted by it*. This creates a direct contradiction, as it is unclear whose license is being terminated in the event of a breach. The contradiction is further shown in the contradicted text, which is the changed text. Therefore, it is the same location in the contract, but it contradicts with the original text, therefore creating a contradiction. This contradiction directly affects the enforceability of the termination clause.
